page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at a base frequency of 3.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with a single thread per core, ensuring that each vCPU maps directly to a dedicated physical core without hyperthreading.

This instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, requiring external block storage for operating system and application data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps, providing consistent throughput for network-dependent workloads.

The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance highly cost-efficient for compute-intensive workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. By dedicating physical cores without SMT, the hardware minimizes resource contention and provides predictable execution times. This profile is suitable for workloads such as high-performance web serving, batch processing, media encoding, and computational modeling.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and utilizing the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a single thread per core to eliminate hyperthreading resource contention. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, offering a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying on external network-attached storage. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that require predictable CPU performance without massive memory overhead, making it suitable for batch processing, web serving, and computational tasks.
